# Security Requirements Quality Checklist: Launcher Core Foundation

**Purpose**: Validate that *security- and privacy-relevant requirements* in the feature spec are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable enough for planning—without checking implementation behavior.  
**Created**: 2026-03-28  
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit.checklist` for domain **security**. `plan.md` was not available; items reference the specification only. Re-run or extend after `/speckit.plan` if integration threats emerge.

**Defaults used**: Depth — Standard; Audience — PR reviewer; Focus — permission/visibility boundaries, data minimization for launcher catalog, trust model for first-party modules, consistency with non-goals.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are requirements documented for how the launcher behaves when **package visibility or launch-related permissions** are denied or restricted, beyond a single edge-case bullet? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK002 Are **data minimization** expectations stated for what **AppIndex** and **catalog entries** may expose to feature modules (e.g., which attributes are launcher-facing vs. sensitive)? [Gap, Spec §Key Entities — Catalog entry, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements defined for **untrusted or malformed profile/configuration input** (integrity and validation expectations), or is that explicitly deferred with a pointer to a future profile spec? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Spec §Non-goals]
- [ ] CHK004 Are **first-party-only modules** and the prohibition of third-party execution documented with enough precision to exclude ambiguous “side-loaded” cases? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014, Spec §Non-goals]
- [ ] CHK005 Is there an explicit statement whether **network-backed** surfaces are **in scope** for Core in MVP, consistent with cloud/analytics non-goals? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Spec §Non-goals, Spec §SC-006]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is **“documented behavior”** for degraded catalog state under permission restrictions defined with enough specificity that reviewers can judge completeness in a future profile/plan artifact? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §Edge Cases — permissions]
- [ ] CHK007 Are **ActionDispatcher** requirements clear about which **classes of actions** are in scope (e.g., launch, settings intents, internal commands) for security review without naming implementation APIs? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Key Entities — Action]
- [ ] CHK008 Does the spec distinguish **security-relevant precedence** (runtime safety first) from **UX precedence** so that requirements are not read as contradicting elderly-first goals? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions — precedence]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK009 Do **centralized OS listener ownership** requirements align with **deduplication** and **no duplicate listeners per feature** without internal tension? [Consistency, Spec §Platform constraints, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §User Story 3]
- [ ] CHK010 Are **non-goals** (no marketplace, no third-party execution) consistent with **optional modules** and **future packaging** assumptions? [Consistency, Spec §Non-goals, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK011 Does the **Favorites extension narrative (FR-026)** remain consistent with **forbidden bypass** of AppIndex, ActionDispatcher, and EventRouter (FR-018)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-026, Spec §FR-018]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK012 Does **SC-006** give reviewers an objective way to confirm absence of cloud/marketplace/analytics scope, or does it rely on informal reading only? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK013 Are **fallback hierarchy** and **conflict-resolution order** expressed so that **security-relevant degradation** (e.g., unsafe profile intent) is covered by **SC-007** without ambiguity? [Measurability, Spec §SC-007, Spec §Assumptions]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK014 Are **primary** flows for partial permission and degraded catalog represented in user stories or edge cases with aligned functional requirements? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK015 Are **exception** scenarios for **contract version incompatibility** tied to safe degradation requirements in a way security reviewers can trace? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases — contract incompatibility, Spec §FR-024]
- [ ] CHK016 Is **recovery** after **invalid or corrupt profile** specified as deterministic fallback, with requirements testable independently of implementation? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-007, Spec §Key Entities — Safe fallback]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK017 Are edge cases listed for **concurrent or conflicting** profile/module/runtime signals sufficient, or is explicit deferral documented for remaining cases? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK018 Is **low memory / process death** addressed only as a generic stability concern, or are **security-relevant implications** (e.g., stale permissions state after restart) called out if needed? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases — low memory]

## Non-Functional Requirements (Security / Privacy)

- [ ] CHK019 Is a **threat model** or **abuse scenario class** (e.g., malicious module in first-party build, hostile profile payload) explicitly in scope, deferred, or intentionally out of scope for this foundation spec? [Gap, Traceability]
- [ ] CHK020 Are **logging, diagnostics, and observability** boundaries stated so they do not accidentally conflict with **no analytics** / **local-first** expectations? [Gap, Spec §FR-013, Spec §Non-goals]
- [ ] CHK021 Are requirements present or explicitly deferred for **sensitive actions** tied to **installed apps and device state** per constitution-style expectations, without turning this spec into an implementation checklist? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Constitution cross-ref]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK022 Is reliance on **“first-party modules only”** documented as an **assumption** that future specs must reinforce if supply-chain or signing policy changes? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK023 Does the spec state whether **accessibility services** or **privileged APIs** are **in or out of scope** for MVP foundation, or is that a documented gap for a follow-on spec? [Gap, Spec §Platform constraints]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK024 Is the term **“published contract”** sufficiently tied to **compatibility and versioning (FR-010)** to avoid security review gaps when contracts evolve? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK025 Could **“god interfaces” (FR-018)** and **contract minimalism (FR-025)** be read as conflicting without guidance on how new cross-cutting security checks should enter the architecture? [Conflict risk, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-025]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Items marked [Gap] may be resolved by updating `spec.md`, a future profile spec, or `plan.md`—not by implementation tickets alone.
